questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A shell is stationary in the free space split in to two fragments of masses 4 kg and 2 kg by it's explosion adds on an energy of 24 J to fragments. The magnitude of relative velocity (in $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$ ) between the two fragments after explosion is: (Assume that energy is released in form of kinetic energy only)
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{2} m_1 v_1^2=E \frac{m_2}{m_1+m_2} \\ & v_1=\sqrt{\frac{2 E}{m_1+m_2} \times \frac{m_2}{m_1}} \\ & v_2=\sqrt{\frac{2 E}{m_1+m_2} \times \frac{m_1}{m_2}} \\ & v_{\text {rel }}=\sqrt{\frac{2 E}{m_1+m_2}} \times \frac{m_1+m_2}{\sqrt{m_1 m_2}}=\sqrt{\frac{2 E\left(m_1+m_2\right)}{m_1 m_2}}-\sqrt{2 \times \frac{24 \times 6}{8}}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A Diwali rocket going vertically up, explodes at the topmost point of its trajectory, at horizontal distance 550 m from the point of projection see figure. When the fragments strike the ground one of the fragments is found at a location 550 m east and 120 m north of the launch point. Second fragment is found at a location 550 m east and 60 m south of the launch point. First two fragments are of equal mass $m$ and third fragment has mass 2 m . All the three fragments struck the ground simultaneously. Take x -axis as east and $y$-axis as north, point of projection as origin. The location of the third fragment with respect to origin when it strikes ground is $x \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\mathrm{y} \hat{\mathrm{j}}$ (in m). Fill $\left|\frac{x}{11 y}\right|$ in your answer sheet.
questions.solution:
Location of COM when they all fall simultaneously $=1100 \mathrm{~m}$ east.
For Y-axis, $\frac{m \times 120-m \times 60+2 m \times y}{4 m}=0$
For X-axis $\frac{m \times 550+m \times 550+2 m \times x}{4 m}=1100$
$\therefore \mathrm{x}=1650, \mathrm{y}=30$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two identical spheres A \& B of mass 50 kg and radius 1 m each lie on a smooth horizontal surface as shown in figure. The initial distance between their centres is 3 m . Starting from $\mathrm{t}=0$ a constant force $\mathrm{F}=100 \mathrm{~N}$ acts on the sphere A towards right. The coefficient of restitution is $e=0.5$. Find the time interval (in sec) between the first collision and the instant after which collision between both the spheres cease.
questions.solution: $T=2\left[\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{\frac{2 R}{F / m}}+\frac{1}{4} \sqrt{\frac{2 R}{(F / m)}}+\frac{1}{8} \sqrt{\frac{2 R}{(F / m)}} \ldots . . \infty\right]=\sqrt{\frac{2 R}{(F / m)}}\left[+1+\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{2^2}+\frac{1}{2^3}+\ldots \ldots . .+\ldots . . \infty\right]$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In the shown figure, a particle of mass $\frac{\mathrm{M}}{10}$ strikes the block of mass M with velocity $\mathrm{v}_0$ and gets attached to it. For what velocity $\mathrm{v}_0$ (in $\mathrm{ms}^{-1}$), the block $B$ just able to leave the ground? (Given $M=100 \mathrm{gm}, \mathrm{K}=880 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}$)
questions.solution: From C.L.M. $\frac{\mathrm{M}}{10} \mathrm{v}_0=\frac{11}{10} \mathrm{Mv} \Rightarrow \mathrm{v}=\frac{\mathrm{v}_0}{11}$ ....(i) For block B to leave ground $\mathrm{K}\left(\mathrm{x}_0+\mathrm{x}\right)=2 \mathrm{Mg}\left(\right.$ where $\left.\mathrm{x}_0=\frac{\mathrm{Mg}}{\mathrm{K}}\right)$ $\therefore \quad x=\frac{\mathrm{Mg}}{\mathrm{K}}$ ....(ii) From COE $\frac{11}{10} \mathrm{Mgx}-\left[\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~K}\left(\mathrm{x}_0+\mathrm{x}\right)^2-\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{Kx}_0^2\right]=0-\frac{1}{2} \frac{11}{10} \mathrm{Mv}^2$ solving $v_0=g \sqrt{\frac{88 \mathrm{M}}{\mathrm{K}}}$ putting the value $\mathrm{v}_0=1 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A student of height $h$ jumps vertically up from the "squat" position. At the top point of the jump, the student's center of mass is at a height $3 \mathrm{~h} / 4$ from the ground. Find the average force F acting on the floor prior to the moment when the student loses contact with the floor. It is known that when the student stands on the floor, the center of mass is at a height $h / 2$ from the floor; in the "squat" position, the center of mass is at a height $\mathrm{h} / 4$ from the floor. The mass of the student is $\mathrm{m}=$ 0.1 kg .(take $\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ )
questions.solution:
Method 1: Even though the normal force with the floor does no physical work on the person (energy transfers occur internally from the muscles), one can write a mathematically correct expression as though the contact force with the floor does change the person's energy. Hence, we can treat the center of mass motion as $W$ net $=\Delta \mathrm{Kcm}$. Here, we are treating it as though only gravity and the floor act onthe person, and since the person is in contact with the floor until the center of mass is a position $h / 2$ from the ground, then $F_{\text {floor }} \frac{h}{4}+(\mathrm{mg})\left(\frac{-h}{2}\right)=0$
as the student starts and ends this motion at rest. Therefore, $\mathrm{F}_{\text {floor }}=2 \mathrm{mg}$.
Method 2: Since the feet come off the floor at a position $\mathrm{h} / 2$ from the ground, then the person accelerates upward from $h / 4$ to $h / 2$ with the same magnitude as the acceleration from $h / 2$ to $3 \mathrm{~h} / 4$ by symmetry. Therefore, the net force on the student going upward is the same in magnitude as the student in free fall from $h / 2$ to $3 \mathrm{~h} / 4$. Hence, while in contact with the floor, using Newton's second law, $F_{\text {floor }}-\mathrm{mg}=\mathrm{mg}=\mathrm{F}_{\text {floor }}=2 \mathrm{mg}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: A thin heavy metal plate is being bombarded by a perpendicular beam of gas particles from both sides as shown in the figure. The solid dots are representing the molecules hitting from left side and the faint dots are the molecules hitting from right side. The mass of these gas particles is $m=10^{-26} \mathrm{~kg}$ and velocity before hitting is $v_0=5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. Volume density of the gas particles on both sides is $\mathrm{n}=10^{25}$ per $\mathrm{m}^3$. Each beam has an area $A=1 \mathrm{~m}^2$ and the collisions are perfectly elastic. What is the external force $F$ (in newton) required to move the plate with a constant velocity $\mathrm{v}=2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The friction coefficient between the horizontal surface and each of the block shown in the figure is 0.2 . The collision between the blocks is perfectly elastic. Find the separation (in cm ) between them when they come to rest. Take $\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$.
questions.solution:
Velocity of first block before collision,
$\begin{aligned}& v_1^2=1^2-2(2) \times 0.16 \\& =1-0.64 \\
& v_1=0.6 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}\end{aligned}$
By conservation of momentum, $2 \times 0.6=2 \mathrm{v}_1^{\prime}+4 \mathrm{v}_2^{\prime}$
also $v_2^{\prime}-v_1^{\prime}=v_1$ for elastic collision
It gives 
$\begin{aligned}& v_2^{\prime}=0.4 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\
& v_1^{\prime}=-0.2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}\end{aligned}$
Now distance moved after collision
$\begin{aligned}& \mathrm{s}_1=\frac{(0.4)^2}{2 \times 2} \& \mathrm{~s}_2=\frac{(0.2)^2}{2 \times 2} \\& \therefore \mathrm{s}=\mathrm{s}_1+\mathrm{s}_2=0.05 \mathrm{~m}=5\mathrm{~cm}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
